key_points: Umbrella review of 9 systematic reviews evaluating laser-activated irrigation (LAI) in endodontics, covering Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG, Nd:YAG, and diode laser systems.
Available evidence on laser-activated irrigation remains limited and heterogeneous; findings across systematic reviews are inconsistent.
Some systematic reviews suggest potential benefits in canal disinfection and postoperative outcomes, but results are not conclusive.
Major sources of heterogeneity include variability in laser wavelengths, study designs, and outcome measures.
Laser-assisted irrigation should currently be regarded as an adjunct with unproven clinical benefit, pending robust evidence from well-designed studies with standardised protocols.
Further standardised, high-quality clinical research is required before definitive conclusions can be established.
Methodological quality of included systematic reviews was assessed using the AMSTAR 2 tool.
Review registered at PROSPERO (CRD42025599352); 5 databases searched through December 2024 with no language restrictions.

outcomes.result: Some systematic reviews suggest potential benefits, but findings are inconsistent across studies
outcomes.clinical_significance: Unproven clinical benefit; evidence is heterogeneous and limited

outcomes.result: Some systematic reviews suggest potential benefits, but results are inconsistent
outcomes.clinical_significance: Cannot draw definitive conclusions due to variability in study designs and outcome measures

notes: This is an umbrella review (review of systematic reviews). Initially 319 systematic reviews identified; 9 ultimately included after exclusion of duplicates, in vitro-only studies, and studies with limited clinical relevance. The review focuses on primary endodontic cases.

population.condition: Primary endodontic cases requiring root canal treatment

source_citation: Duarte, M. A., Neves, J. A., Caçador, M., & Flores-Fraile, J. (2026). Clinical Efficiency of Lasers in Endodontic Treatment of Primary Endodontic Cases: An Umbrella Review of Systematic Reviews. International Dental Journal. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.identj.2026.109470
clinical_indication: Root canal irrigation and disinfection in primary endodontic cases
target_tissue: root canal (pulp chamber and canal walls)
uncertainties: No specific laser parameters (wavelength settings, power, fluence, pulse duration) are reported in the abstract; the review aggregates findings across multiple laser types without protocol-level detail.
Conflicts of interest and funding sources not declared in the available abstract.
AMSTAR 2 quality ratings for individual included systematic reviews not detailed in the abstract.
Number of patients across included studies not reported in the abstract.
